terms.definition: Refractive error in which distant objects appear blurred because images focus in front of the retina.
terms.exampleSentence: The young patient's progressive myopia was managed with low-dose atropine and orthokeratology.
terms.contextNotes: High myopia (>6 D) increases retinal detachment risk.

terms.definition: Refractive error in which near objects appear blurred because images focus behind the retina.
terms.exampleSentence: Uncorrected hyperopia was contributing to his eye strain during prolonged reading.
terms.contextNotes: Children may compensate via accommodation, masking hyperopia.

terms.definition: Age-related loss of accommodation causing difficulty focusing on near objects, typically from the mid-forties.
terms.exampleSentence: Presbyopia was managed with progressive addition spectacle lenses.
terms.contextNotes: Caused by lens stiffening, not corneal change.

terms.definition: Refractive error from non-spherical corneal or lenticular curvature, causing blurred or distorted vision at all distances.
terms.exampleSentence: Regular corneal astigmatism was corrected with toric soft contact lenses.
terms.contextNotes: Irregular astigmatism may indicate keratoconus.

terms.definition: Significant difference in refractive error between the two eyes, often defined as ≥1 D.
terms.exampleSentence: Childhood anisometropia of 2 D had led to amblyopia in the more hyperopic eye.
terms.contextNotes: Risk factor for amblyopia; consider contact lens correction.

terms.definition: Refractive state in which parallel light rays focus precisely on the retina without accommodation.
terms.exampleSentence: Post-LASIK refraction confirmed emmetropia with 6/6 unaided vision.
terms.contextNotes: Used as the post-refractive surgery target.

terms.definition: Opacification of the crystalline lens leading to progressive painless visual loss.
terms.exampleSentence: A dense nuclear cataract was reducing visual acuity to 6/24 in the right eye.
terms.contextNotes: Refer for surgery when symptoms affect daily function.

terms.definition: Chronic optic neuropathy with progressive RNFL loss and characteristic field defects, despite an open iridocorneal angle.
terms.exampleSentence: Selective laser trabeculoplasty was offered as first-line treatment for early open-angle glaucoma.
terms.contextNotes: NICE recommends 24-2 perimetry and OCT RNFL monitoring.

terms.definition: Acute or chronic glaucoma caused by apposition of the iris against the trabecular meshwork, blocking aqueous outflow.
terms.exampleSentence: Acute angle-closure glaucoma presented with a painful red eye, nausea and haloes around lights.
terms.contextNotes: Ophthalmic emergency — refer same day.

terms.definition: Degenerative macular disease causing central vision loss; classified as dry (atrophic) or wet (neovascular).
terms.exampleSentence: Anti-VEGF injections were initiated for active wet age-related macular degeneration.
terms.contextNotes: Amsler grid useful for self-monitoring.

terms.definition: Microvascular retinal complication of diabetes, ranging from background changes to proliferative disease with neovascularisation.
terms.exampleSentence: Annual screening detected moderate non-proliferative diabetic retinopathy.
terms.contextNotes: NHS Diabetic Eye Screening Programme is annual.

terms.definition: Separation of the neurosensory retina from the underlying RPE, threatening permanent vision loss.
terms.exampleSentence: Sudden onset of flashes and a curtain in the inferior field suggested retinal detachment.
terms.contextNotes: Macula-on RD is a same-day surgical emergency.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the conjunctiva from infection, allergy or irritants, causing redness and discharge.
terms.exampleSentence: Bilateral mucopurulent discharge was consistent with bacterial conjunctivitis.
terms.contextNotes: Most viral cases self-resolve in 1-2 weeks.

terms.definition: Chronic inflammation of the eyelid margins, often associated with Meibomian gland dysfunction or rosacea.
terms.exampleSentence: Lid hygiene with warm compresses and dilute baby shampoo was advised for chronic blepharitis.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel patients that treatment is long-term, not curative.

terms.definition: Multifactorial disease of the ocular surface with loss of tear film homeostasis, causing irritation and visual disturbance.
terms.exampleSentence: Reduced tear break-up time confirmed evaporative dry eye disease.
terms.contextNotes: TFOS DEWS II classifies aqueous-deficient vs evaporative.

terms.definition: Reduced best-corrected visual acuity in one or both eyes due to abnormal visual experience in early childhood.
terms.exampleSentence: Patching therapy was started for moderate anisometropic amblyopia in the left eye.
terms.contextNotes: Best treated before about age 7.

terms.definition: Misalignment of the visual axes such that both eyes do not fixate simultaneously on the same target.
terms.exampleSentence: Cover-uncover testing revealed an intermittent exotropic strabismus.
terms.contextNotes: Sudden adult strabismus warrants urgent neurology workup.

terms.definition: Progressive non-inflammatory thinning and conical protrusion of the cornea causing irregular astigmatism.
terms.exampleSentence: Corneal cross-linking was offered to halt the progression of keratoconus.
terms.contextNotes: Suspect in young patients with rapidly changing astigmatism.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the uveal tract, classified anatomically as anterior, intermediate, posterior or panuveitis.
terms.exampleSentence: Anterior uveitis presented with photophobia, cells in the AC and a constricted pupil.
terms.contextNotes: Look for systemic associations (HLA-B27, sarcoid, IBD).

terms.definition: Severe painful inflammation of the sclera, frequently associated with systemic autoimmune disease.
terms.exampleSentence: Deep boring eye pain that woke the patient at night was characteristic of scleritis.
terms.contextNotes: Differentiate from milder, self-limiting episcleritis.

terms.definition: Mobile opacities in the vitreous perceived as drifting spots, threads or cobwebs in the visual field.
terms.exampleSentence: Sudden onset of new floaters with photopsia warranted urgent dilated fundus examination.
terms.contextNotes: Sudden showers of floaters need same-day review.

terms.definition: Abnormal sensitivity to light causing discomfort or pain on exposure to normal lighting.
terms.exampleSentence: Marked photophobia and a small pupil were highly suggestive of anterior uveitis.
terms.contextNotes: Red flag if combined with neck stiffness — meningitis.

terms.definition: Perception of two images of a single object, monocular if one eye affected, binocular if both eyes open.
terms.exampleSentence: Binocular diplopia resolved on covering either eye, suggesting an extraocular muscle palsy.
terms.contextNotes: New binocular diplopia needs urgent neurology assessment.

terms.definition: Involuntary rhythmic oscillation of the eyes, which may be congenital, acquired, jerk or pendular.
terms.exampleSentence: Horizontal jerk nystagmus on right gaze prompted MRI to exclude posterior fossa pathology.
terms.contextNotes: New-onset adult nystagmus is a red flag.

terms.definition: Drooping of the upper eyelid below its normal position, which may be congenital or acquired.
terms.exampleSentence: New unilateral ptosis with a dilated pupil raised concern for a third nerve palsy.
terms.contextNotes: Painful ptosis with pupil involvement = aneurysm until proven otherwise.

terms.definition: Painless granulomatous lid lump caused by chronic obstruction and inflammation of a Meibomian gland.
terms.exampleSentence: A persistent chalazion was scheduled for incision and curettage.
terms.contextNotes: Recurrent atypical chalazia require biopsy to exclude carcinoma.

terms.definition: Acute tender infection of an eyelash follicle or associated gland, typically by Staphylococcus aureus.
terms.exampleSentence: A painful external stye was managed with warm compresses and lid hygiene.
terms.contextNotes: Antibiotic only if surrounding cellulitis develops.

terms.definition: Traumatic loss of corneal epithelium causing pain, photophobia and a foreign-body sensation.
terms.exampleSentence: Fluorescein staining confirmed a linear corneal abrasion from a fingernail injury.
terms.contextNotes: Prophylactic topical antibiotic; never patch contact lens-related abrasions.

terms.definition: Inherited progressive rod-cone dystrophy causing nyctalopia, peripheral field loss and pigmentary fundus changes.
terms.exampleSentence: Bone-spicule pigmentation in the mid-periphery was diagnostic of retinitis pigmentosa.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel about driving, mobility aids and genetic implications.

terms.definition: Idiopathic detachment of the neurosensory retina at the macula due to RPE leakage, often in young men.
terms.exampleSentence: OCT revealed subretinal fluid at the fovea consistent with central serous retinopathy.
terms.contextNotes: Strongly linked to corticosteroid use and stress.
